In addition to the efficient use of energy, renewable energy sources are considered the most important pillar of a sustainable energy policy and the energy transition.
Secondary energy sources (electricity, heat, fuel) derived from renewable energies are also referred to as renewable energies.
Change in the energy system The transformation from conventional energy supply to renewable energy changes the structure of the energy industry massively.

On the other hand, pumped storage power plants and above all power-to-gas plants with biogas and hydrogen must be driven up in phases where too little wind and solar energy are produced, as well as electricity storage systems.
Intelligent electricity consumption The construction of smart grids plays an important role in the conversion of electricity supply.
Only then will benefits and damage in the energy and entropy balance, for biodiversity and social consequences become clear.
